content.problemStatement:
You are given a matrix of integers AAA with NNN rows (numbered 111 through NNN) and MMM columns (numbered 111 through MMM). Each element of this matrix is either 000 or 111.

A move consists of the following steps:


Choose two different rows r1r_1r1‚Äã and r2r_2r2‚Äã or two different columns c1c_1c1‚Äã and c2c_2c2‚Äã.
Apply the bitwise OR operation with the second row/column on the first row/column. Formally, if you chose two rows, this means you should change Ar1,kA_{r_1, k}Ar1‚Äã,k‚Äã to Ar1,k‚à®Ar2,kA_{r_1, k} \lor A_{r_2, k}Ar1‚Äã,k‚Äã‚à®Ar2‚Äã,k‚Äã for each 1‚â§k‚â§M1 \le k \le M1‚â§k‚â§M; if you chose two columns, then you should change Ak,c1A_{k, c_1}Ak,c1‚Äã‚Äã to Ak,c1‚à®Ak,c2A_{k, c_1} \lor A_{k, c_2}Ak,c1‚Äã‚Äã‚à®Ak,c2‚Äã‚Äã for each 1‚â§k‚â§N1 \le k \le N1‚â§k‚â§N.
For each element of the matrix, compute the minimum number of moves required to make it equal to 111 or determine that it is impossible. Note that these answers are independent, i.e. we are starting with the initial matrix for each of them.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
NNN lines follow. For each iii (1‚â§i‚â§N1 \le i \le N1‚â§i‚â§N), the iii-th of these lines contains MMM integers Ai,1,Ai,2,‚Ä¶,Ai,MA_{i, 1}, A_{i, 2}, \dots, A_{i, M}Ai,1‚Äã,Ai,2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,Ai,M‚Äã NOT separated by spaces.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case, print NNN lines. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines should contain MMM space-separated integers; for each valid jjj, the jjj-th of these integers should be the minimum number of moves required to make Ai,jA_{i, j}Ai,j‚Äã equal to 111, or ‚àí1-1‚àí1 if it is impossible.

###Constraints


1‚â§T‚â§1001 \le T \le 1001‚â§T‚â§100
1‚â§N,M‚â§1,0001 \le N, M \le 1,0001‚â§N,M‚â§1,000
Ai,j‚àà{0,1}A_{i, j} \in \{0, 1\}Ai,j‚Äã‚àà{0,1} for each valid i,ji, ji,j
the sum of N‚ãÖMN \cdot MN‚ãÖM for all test cases does not exceed 1,000,0001,000,0001,000,000
